metadata.archetype_justification: MongoDB is the primary persistent data store with full CRUD lifecycle management. The application exposes a comprehensive REST API with create/update/delete operations on business entities.

findings.title: No Managed Compute Infrastructure
findings.description: No managed compute (ECS/EKS/Lambda/Fargate) defined. No IaC for any compute resources. Application is packaged for self-managed deployment via Sonatype Nexus.
findings.gap: All compute is self-managed with no managed container orchestration or serverless adoption.
findings.recommendation: Containerize the application and deploy on Amazon EKS. Create a production Dockerfile, define Kubernetes manifests with StatefulSets for journal-dependent components.

findings.title: All Databases Self-Managed
findings.description: MongoDB referenced via localhost connection string. OpenSearch managed by custom data-node module. Docker-compose defines self-hosted MongoDB 7.0 and OpenSearch 2.10. No managed database services configured.
findings.gap: All databases self-managed creating operational burden for patching, backup, scaling, and failover.
findings.recommendation: Migrate MongoDB to Amazon DocumentDB and OpenSearch to Amazon OpenSearch Service for automated backups, Multi-AZ failover, and managed patching.

findings.title: Workflows Hardcoded in Custom Framework
findings.description: Multi-step workflows (migration sequences, index rotation, event correlation) implemented as hardcoded state machines in the custom Periodical framework with 26+ scheduled background tasks.
findings.gap: No dedicated orchestration service provides visibility, retry logic, or error handling beyond custom code.
findings.recommendation: Adopt AWS Step Functions for multi-step operations like index rotation, data migration, and event correlation workflows. EventBridge can trigger workflows based on system events.

findings.title: Cross-Node Events Use MongoDB Polling
findings.description: Kafka 4.2.0 and AMQP used as external input transports. Internal cross-node cluster events use MongoDB-polled ClusterEventBus rather than managed messaging.
findings.gap: ClusterEventBus uses MongoDB polling with latency and MongoDB coupling instead of managed messaging.
findings.recommendation: Migrate ClusterEventBus from MongoDB polling to Amazon EventBridge or SQS for decoupled, low-latency event propagation.

findings.title: No Network Security Configuration
findings.description: No VPC, subnet, security group, or NACL definitions. No IaC defining network topology. TLS disabled by default in configuration.
findings.gap: No network segmentation, no VPC configuration, no security groups. TLS disabled by default.
findings.recommendation: Define VPC infrastructure with private subnets for all components. Use security groups with least-privilege rules. Enable TLS by default. Consider VPC endpoints for AWS service access.

findings.title: No Managed API Entry Point
findings.description: No API Gateway, ALB, CloudFront, or managed entry point configured. REST API exposed directly on configurable ports.
findings.gap: Services exposed directly with no managed gateway. No throttling, request validation, or centralized auth at entry point.
findings.recommendation: Deploy behind API Gateway for external access or ALB for internal. API Gateway provides throttling, request validation, and centralized authentication.

findings.title: No Auto-Scaling Configuration
findings.description: No auto-scaling configuration exists. Application supports clustering but scaling is entirely manual.
findings.gap: All capacity is statically provisioned with no automatic response to traffic changes.
findings.recommendation: Configure HPA on EKS based on custom Prometheus metrics (journal utilization, input buffer usage). Configure auto-scaling on DocumentDB read replicas.

findings.title: No Backup Configuration
findings.description: No backup configuration in the repository. No AWS Backup plans, no snapshot policies, no backup retention settings.
findings.gap: No backup configuration found. Data recovery depends on external procedures not defined here.
findings.recommendation: Enable automated backups on DocumentDB (PITR), snapshot policies on OpenSearch Service, and define an AWS Backup plan with cross-region replication.

findings.title: No Multi-AZ Configuration
findings.description: No Multi-AZ configuration exists. Cluster architecture supports HA in principle but no infrastructure definition ensures AZ distribution.
findings.gap: No AZ configuration found despite multi-instance cluster architecture.
findings.recommendation: Configure pod anti-affinity rules on EKS to spread across AZs. Use Multi-AZ DocumentDB. Amazon OpenSearch Service provides Multi-AZ by default.

findings.title: Zero Infrastructure as Code
findings.description: No Terraform, CloudFormation, CDK, Helm charts, or Kustomize files found. All infrastructure is created and managed externally.
findings.gap: No IaC — all infrastructure created outside this repository with no reproducibility.
findings.recommendation: Create IaC (Terraform or AWS CDK) defining EKS cluster, DocumentDB, OpenSearch Service, VPC/networking, ALB/API Gateway, IAM roles, CloudWatch alarms, and backup plans.

findings.title: CI Automation Without Deployment
findings.description: GitHub Actions provides CI with 4 parallel jobs (build, frontend-tests, backend-tests, full-backend-tests). No deployment automation exists.
findings.gap: Build is automated but deployment is manual. No CD pipeline to production.
findings.recommendation: Extend GitHub Actions with deploy stages: build container → push to ECR → deploy to EKS staging → smoke tests → promote to production via ArgoCD.

findings.title: Modular Monolith with Shared Database
findings.description: 40+ domain packages with plugin architecture via Guice modules. Modules share single MongoDB instance with cross-module collection access, in-process Guava EventBus, and LMAX Disruptor ring buffers.
findings.gap: Shared database schemas, shared in-process communication, no independent deployability of modules.
findings.recommendation: Apply Strangler Fig pattern to extract high-value modules (alerting/events, pipeline processing, archiving) into independent services. Start with per-module database schemas and external events.

findings.title: Primarily Synchronous Communication
findings.description: ProxiedResource pattern fans out HTTP requests synchronously to all cluster nodes. Cross-node events use MongoDB-polled ClusterEventBus. In-process Guava EventBus is synchronous dispatch.
findings.gap: Primarily synchronous with synchronous HTTP fan-out creating cascading failure risk when nodes are unhealthy.
findings.recommendation: Replace synchronous cluster fan-out with async messaging via EventBridge or SQS for cross-node state changes. Keep synchronous reads for configuration queries.

findings.title: Inconsistent Long-Running Process Handling
findings.description: Custom Periodical framework with 30-thread ScheduledThreadPoolExecutor handles background tasks. Job scheduler has MongoDB-backed status. No unified async job API with status polling.
findings.gap: Background job processing exists but with inconsistent patterns and no status polling API for operations exceeding 30 seconds.
findings.recommendation: Implement async job status API for long-running operations. Adopt Step Functions for multi-step workflows with visibility and automatic retries.

findings.title: No API Versioning Strategy
findings.description: REST API uses flat /api/ prefix with domain-based paths. No /v1/, /v2/ URL patterns, no Accept-Version headers, no versioning annotations.
findings.gap: No versioning applied — API changes deployed directly without backward compatibility guarantees.
findings.recommendation: Introduce API versioning using URL path prefixes (/api/v1/) for external-facing endpoints. Use API Gateway stage-based versioning.

findings.title: Custom MongoDB-Based Service Discovery
findings.description: Nodes register HTTP URIs in MongoDB collection. Peers discovered by querying MongoDB. Leader election uses MongoDB distributed locks. No standard discovery mechanism.
findings.gap: All service endpoints registered in MongoDB with no dynamic discovery. Couples discovery to database.
findings.recommendation: After migrating to EKS, leverage Kubernetes Service discovery (DNS-based). Use AWS Cloud Map for external service integration.

findings.title: Primary Data in Search Engine, Not S3
findings.description: Log data stored in OpenSearch/Elasticsearch indices. S3 integration exists for archiving with Iceberg table format. Active dataset requires search engine for access.
findings.gap: Data in managed storage but not S3 for primary log store. No automated parsing pipeline for archived data.
findings.recommendation: Leverage existing S3 archiver with Iceberg format. Add Athena for querying archived data without restoring to OpenSearch.

findings.title: Mostly Centralized Data Access
findings.description: MongoDB access centralized through MongoJack with entity-level service classes. Some direct access exists in migration classes and auxiliary code paths.
findings.gap: Mostly centralized with some direct access in migrations and auxiliary code.
findings.recommendation: Maintain the current pattern. When extracting microservices, ensure each service gets its own data access layer with clearly defined collection ownership.

findings.title: Versions Pinned in CI Only
findings.description: MongoDB 7.0/8.0 and OpenSearch 2.19.3 specified in docker-compose and CI matrix. Current versions with no EOL risk. No production IaC pinning.
findings.gap: Versions pinned in CI/test but no production IaC exists to enforce version pinning. No documented upgrade procedure.
findings.recommendation: When creating IaC for managed databases, explicitly pin engine versions. Define a version upgrade procedure document.

findings.title: Minimal MongoDB-Specific Coupling
findings.description: No stored procedures or triggers. All business logic in Java application layer. Some MongoDB aggregation pipelines used for performance-critical queries.
findings.gap: Minimal MongoDB aggregation pipeline usage creates minor engine coupling requiring validation for DocumentDB compatibility.
findings.recommendation: Verify MongoDB aggregation pipelines are compatible with Amazon DocumentDB. Run the DocumentDB compatibility assessment tool before migration.

findings.title: No CloudTrail Audit Logging
findings.description: No CloudTrail or equivalent infrastructure audit logging configured. OpenSearch security audit exists but covers only the OpenSearch layer.
findings.gap: No infrastructure-level audit trail. No immutable log storage.
findings.recommendation: Enable CloudTrail with log file validation and immutable storage (S3 Object Lock). Route CloudTrail logs into the Graylog instance for centralized audit analysis.

findings.title: No Encryption at Rest
findings.description: No encryption at rest configuration. No KMS keys. Docker-compose runs databases without encryption. Only application-level EncryptedValue mechanism for stored credentials.
findings.gap: No encryption at rest for MongoDB data, OpenSearch indices, or Kafka journal.
findings.recommendation: Enable encryption at rest on DocumentDB (KMS), OpenSearch Service (KMS), and EBS volumes. Define customer-managed KMS keys with rotation policy.

findings.title: Token-Based Auth Present but Non-Standard
findings.description: Apache Shiro with JWT provides authentication on all endpoints. Mixed session-based and token-based model. No standard OAuth2 flow.
findings.gap: Proprietary token mechanism. No OAuth2/OIDC standard flow. Internal cluster communication may bypass auth.
findings.recommendation: Standardize on OAuth2/OIDC when deploying behind API Gateway. Use API Gateway authorizers (Cognito or custom) for external surface.

findings.title: LDAP/AD Federation Without Modern SSO
findings.description: LDAP and Active Directory integration via authservice framework. Internal MongoDB-backed user store as primary. No SAML, OIDC, or OAuth2 SSO in open-source codebase.
findings.gap: Can federate with LDAP/AD but no modern SSO protocols (SAML, OIDC, OAuth2) for centralized identity.
findings.recommendation: Integrate with Amazon Cognito as centralized IdP with OIDC/SAML support. Cognito can federate with existing LDAP/AD backends.

findings.title: No External Secrets Manager
findings.description: No AWS Secrets Manager or HashiCorp Vault integration. Production credentials expected in environment variables or config files. Internal EncryptedValue mechanism exists but without rotation.
findings.gap: No external secrets manager. No rotation configured. Production credentials in env vars without centralized management.
findings.recommendation: Integrate AWS Secrets Manager for all production credentials. Configure automated rotation for database credentials. Use EKS CSI Secrets Store driver.

findings.title: Default Images Without Hardening
findings.description: Test Dockerfile uses eclipse-temurin:21-jre-jammy. Dependabot provides dependency scanning. Maven includes CVE mitigations. No container image scanning or runtime vulnerability scanning.
findings.gap: Default base images with no hardening. No container image scanning or runtime vulnerability assessment.
findings.recommendation: Use hardened base image (Amazon Corretto on Amazon Linux 2023 or Bottlerocket for EKS nodes). Enable ECR image scanning. Add AWS Inspector.

findings.title: Dependency Scanning Present but SAST Skipped
findings.description: Dependabot active with 8 ecosystems. SpotBugs and PMD configured but explicitly skipped in CI. Error Prone provides compile-time detection. No container scanning.
findings.gap: Dependency scanning runs but SAST tools (SpotBugs) are skipped in CI. No blocking security gate.
findings.recommendation: Enable SpotBugs in CI. Add ECR image scanning. Configure a security gate blocking on critical findings. Consider Semgrep for additional SAST.

findings.title: Basic Tracing Without Cross-Service Propagation
findings.description: OpenTelemetry Java Agent support with TracerProvider and custom semantic attributes. No explicit span creation in production code. Cross-service trace propagation not implemented.
findings.gap: Basic tracing on individual nodes but no custom spans for business operations and no cross-node trace propagation.
findings.recommendation: Add explicit span creation for key business operations. Propagate trace context in ProxiedResource HTTP headers. Configure X-Ray or Managed Grafana as trace backend.

findings.title: No SLO Definitions
findings.description: No SLO definitions, error budget tracking, or availability targets. 200+ Prometheus metrics available for measurement but no SLO definitions exist.
findings.gap: No SLOs — no formal definition of acceptable service levels for log ingestion, search, or availability.
findings.recommendation: Define SLOs for critical user journeys: log ingestion latency (p99), search response time (p99), API availability. Implement error budget tracking with Managed Grafana.

findings.title: Business Metrics Available but No Dashboards
findings.description: 200+ Prometheus metrics include business-relevant data: stream message rates, journal utilization, pipeline execution times, event processing, traffic throughput, data lake I/O.
findings.gap: Business metrics tracked but no dashboards defined in the repository. Not systematically organized across all features.
findings.recommendation: Define Grafana dashboards (as code) for existing business metrics. Add metrics for remaining features. Use Amazon Managed Grafana.

findings.title: No Alerting for Self-Monitoring
findings.description: No operational alerts for Graylog's own health. No CloudWatch alarms, Prometheus alerting rules, or PagerDuty integration for self-monitoring despite being an alerting platform for others.
findings.gap: No alerting configured for operational health. No anomaly detection on error rates or latency.
findings.recommendation: Define Prometheus alerting rules for journal utilization, search latency, node health, input failures. Configure CloudWatch Anomaly Detection on error rates.

findings.title: No Deployment Strategy
findings.description: No deployment automation. CI builds and tests only. No blue/green, canary, or rolling deployment. Releases published to Sonatype Nexus. Actual deployment is manual.
findings.gap: Direct-to-production deployment with no staged rollout.
findings.recommendation: Implement canary deployments on EKS using ArgoCD Rollouts with progressive traffic shifting and automatic rollback on error rate increase.

findings.title: Strong Integration Tests in CI
findings.description: 92+ integration tests using Testcontainers with real MongoDB, OpenSearch, S3. 50 E2E tests in full-backend-tests module. All run in CI pipeline.
findings.gap: Integration tests for primary workflows exist. Some gaps in coverage. No contract tests between cluster nodes.
findings.recommendation: Add contract tests for inter-node API (RemoteInterface contracts). Expand full-backend-tests for deployment scenarios. Current coverage is strong.

findings.title: No Incident Response Automation
findings.description: No runbooks, incident playbooks, SSM Automation documents, or self-healing patterns. Incident response is entirely ad hoc.
findings.gap: No runbooks — incident response entirely ad hoc with no documentation or automation.
findings.recommendation: Create operational runbooks for common incidents (journal full, search degraded, node unresponsive). Define SSM Automation for automated remediation.

findings.title: No Observability Ownership
findings.description: No CODEOWNERS for observability assets. No per-service dashboards defined. No named owners on alarms. Prometheus metrics have no ownership metadata.
findings.gap: No observability ownership — monitoring assets have no defined owners or team attribution.
findings.recommendation: Define CODEOWNERS for observability configs. Create per-component dashboards with named team owners. Attribute SLOs to specific teams.

findings.title: No Resource Tagging
findings.description: No resource tagging patterns found. No IaC with tags. No tagging standard, cost allocation, or ownership attribution via tags.
findings.gap: No tags on resources. No tagging standard.
findings.recommendation: Define mandatory tagging schema (Environment, Service, Team, CostCenter, DataClassification). Use default_tags in Terraform. Enforce via AWS Config and Tag Policies.

top_gaps.title: Managed Compute
top_gaps.gap: No managed compute — all self-managed deployment
top_gaps.impact: Prevents elastic scaling, increases operational overhead

top_gaps.title: Audit Logging
top_gaps.gap: No CloudTrail or infrastructure audit logging
top_gaps.impact: No forensic analysis capability, compliance risk

top_gaps.title: SLO Definitions
top_gaps.gap: No SLOs defined for any user journey
top_gaps.impact: Cannot measure service commitments or prioritize reliability

top_gaps.title: Deployment Strategy
top_gaps.gap: No deployment automation or staged rollout
top_gaps.impact: Manual deployments with full user impact on failures

quick_agent_wins.name: Data Query Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: DATA-Q2 >= 2 (score: 3). Centralized MongoJack data access layer with entity services.
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: Natural language querying of operational data via existing MCP server tools (SearchMessagesTool, AggregateMessagesTool, ListStreamsTool).
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: MCP server already implemented. Win is partially realized.

quick_agent_wins.name: RAG-based Knowledge Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: Documentation exists (docs/ directory, changelog/ fragments, OpenAPI spec).
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: Developer/operator knowledge assistant for configuration, troubleshooting, and architecture questions.
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Index documentation into vector store. MCP infrastructure provides serving layer.

quick_agent_wins.name: Observability Agent
quick_agent_wins.prerequisite: OPS-Q1 >= 2 (score: 2). OpenTelemetry javaagent support with 200+ Prometheus metrics.
quick_agent_wins.what_it_enables: Agent that queries metrics, correlates with log patterns, and suggests root causes.
quick_agent_wins.additional_steps: Configure OTel javaagent in production. Set up Amazon Managed Prometheus as metrics store.

decomposition_strategy.rationale: Graylog has identifiable modules with shared MongoDB access. Strangler Fig allows incremental extraction while monolith continues serving core log management.
decomposition_strategy.effort_estimate: Medium to High — 6-12 months for first 2-3 service extractions

immediate_actions: Identify module boundaries for first extraction
Replace in-process EventBus with external events

pathways.name: Move to Containers
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q1=1, no production container definitions found

immediate_actions: Create production Dockerfile from test Dockerfile

pathways.name: Move to Open Source
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: DATA-Q4=3 (above threshold). No commercial DB engines detected — MongoDB and OpenSearch are already open source.

triggering_questions.note: Score meets threshold; no commercial engines detected

pathways.not_triggered_reason: MongoDB and OpenSearch are open-source database engines. No commercial (Oracle, SQL Server) engines detected.

immediate_actions: Assess DocumentDB compatibility for MongoDB aggregation pipelines
Plan MongoDB to DocumentDB migration using DMS
Replace data-node with Amazon OpenSearch Service

pathways.name: Move to Managed Analytics
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: INF-Q4=3 (meets threshold). No data pipeline/ETL workloads detected in repository.

triggering_questions.note: Score meets threshold; no ETL/analytics workloads detected

pathways.not_triggered_reason: No data processing workloads (ETL, data pipelines, analytics jobs) found in the repository. Contextual guard prevents triggering.

immediate_actions: Add deploy stages to GitHub Actions
Implement canary deployments with ArgoCD Rollouts

pathways.name: Move to AI
pathways.key_trigger_criteria: AI/agent frameworks ARE present (MCP 1.1.0 with full server implementation including tools, resources, and configuration).
pathways.not_triggered_reason: AI/agent frameworks already present — MCP 1.1.0 with full server implementation (SearchMessagesTool, AggregateMessagesTool, ListStreamsTool, etc.). Primary trigger condition not met.
